seo.title: Technical SEO for Web Apps implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Technical SEO for Web Apps projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides specific, technical verification steps to ensure web applications are optimized for search engine crawlers, meet Core Web Vitals standards, and implement structured data correctly before moving to production.

items.title: Server-Side Rendering (SSR) Verification
items.description: Disable JavaScript in the browser and verify that all primary page content and navigation links remain visible in the source HTML.

items.title: Self-Referencing Canonical Tags
items.description: Confirm every indexable URL contains a <link rel="canonical"> tag pointing to its own absolute URL to prevent duplicate content issues.

items.title: Robots.txt Configuration
items.description: Verify the robots.txt file is accessible at the root, includes a link to the XML sitemap, and does not block critical CSS or JS assets.

items.title: HTTP Status Code Accuracy
items.description: Ensure non-existent routes return a true 404 status code rather than a 200 OK with a 'Not Found' message.

items.title: Noindex Tag Audit
items.description: Check that the <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> tag is removed from all production pages that were previously restricted during staging.

items.title: Unique Title Tag Implementation
items.description: Verify every route has a unique <title> tag between 50-60 characters that accurately describes the page content.

items.title: Meta Description Length and Content
items.description: Ensure every page has a unique meta description under 155 characters that contains relevant keywords without keyword stuffing.

items.title: JSON-LD Schema Validation
items.description: Test all structured data blocks (Product, Article, FAQ, etc.) using the Schema Markup Validator to ensure zero syntax errors.

items.title: Open Graph and Twitter Card Tags
items.description: Verify og:title, og:description, og:image, and twitter:card tags are present and point to valid assets for social sharing.

items.title: H1 Heading Hierarchy
items.description: Confirm each page contains exactly one <h1> tag that represents the primary topic of the page.

items.title: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) Optimization
items.description: Preload the LCP image using <link rel="preload"> and ensure it is not lazy-loaded via the loading="lazy" attribute.

items.title: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) Prevention
items.description: Set explicit width and height attributes on all and

items.title: Font Display Strategy
items.description: Apply font-display: swap in CSS @font-face declarations to prevent invisible text during font loading.

items.title: Image Format and Compression
items.description: Serve images in Next-Gen formats (WebP or AVIF) and ensure they are compressed to under 200KB where possible.

items.title: Minification of Assets
items.description: Verify that all production CSS and JavaScript files are minified and bundled to reduce total byte size.

items.title: Standard Link Implementation
items.description: Ensure all internal navigation uses standard <a href="..."> tags instead of JavaScript-based click handlers to allow crawler discovery.

items.title: XML Sitemap Generation
items.description: Automate the generation of a dynamic sitemap.xml file that includes all indexable URLs and excludes 404s or redirected pages.

items.title: Trailing Slash Consistency
items.description: Enforce a single convention for trailing slashes (either always on or always off) and implement 301 redirects to the preferred version.

items.title: Breadcrumb Schema Implementation
items.description: Verify that breadcrumb navigation is present and marked up with BreadcrumbList JSON-LD for rich snippet eligibility.

items.title: Broken Link Audit
items.description: Run a crawl using Screaming Frog or a similar tool to identify and fix any internal 404 errors or redirect loops.

items.title: Hydration Error Check
items.description: Monitor the console for React/Next.js hydration mismatches that can cause the DOM to be rewritten incorrectly for crawlers.

items.title: Infinite Scroll Accessibility
items.description: Provide a paginated alternative or use the History API to ensure content loaded via infinite scroll has a discoverable URL.

items.title: Lazy-Loaded Content Visibility
items.description: Verify that content loaded via Intersection Observer or similar APIs is still accessible to crawlers that do not trigger scroll events.

items.title: Third-Party Script Impact
items.description: Audit the impact of third-party scripts (GTM, Hotjar) on Interaction to Next Paint (INP) and defer non-critical scripts.

items.title: Fragment Identifier Handling
items.description: Ensure that critical content is not hidden behind hash fragments (#) as search engines generally ignore content following the hash.
